style_notes.note: Tutu's own carousel style uses dark backgrounds with centered, bold text. Minimal imagery on text slides. Signature branding with her crest/logo.
High-performing carousels in Tutu's space use a photo-first cover slide with bold text overlay to stop the scroll, then switch to cleaner text slides for the teaching content.
Two dominant visual styles Tutu gravitates toward: (1) Dark/moody with gold accents, premium editorial feel. (2) Clean light backgrounds with photo at top and structured text below.
Hooks that work in Tutu's space: provocative statements that challenge assumptions, numbered lists, and strong emotional openers about struggle/growth.
Tutu's carousel captions are long-form and narrative. She tells personal stories, shares lessons learned, and always ties back to the bigger mission.
Font feel: premium serif or editorial sans-serif. Never playful or casual. Think high-end magazine editorial, not social media template.
CTA patterns: Final slide always has a clear action. Tutu should always drive to email list or YouTube.
Slide count sweet spot: 5-8 slides for educational/tips. 8-12 for story-driven or motivational. Tutu tends toward the longer end.
NEVER use em dashes in any carousel text. Use commas, semicolons, colons, or full stops instead. Hard rule from Tutu.
One idea per slide with generous white/dark space. No cluttered slides. Each slide readable in 2-3 seconds.
